howItWorks.description: GAIA monitors Discord for your configured triggers and creates Todoist tasks instantly. Completions flow back to Discord so the whole team stays informed.

faqs.question: Do Discord members need a Todoist account to create tasks?
faqs.answer: No. Discord members trigger task creation through GAIA's Discord bot. Only the GAIA account connected to Todoist needs a valid Todoist login.

faqs.question: Can I assign tasks to specific Todoist projects based on the Discord channel?
faqs.answer: Yes. GAIA's channel-to-project mapping lets you route tasks from different Discord channels into separate Todoist projects automatically. You configure the mapping once in GAIA's settings.

faqs.question: Will GAIA create duplicate tasks if the same message is reacted to twice?
faqs.answer: GAIA deduplicates by tracking which messages have already generated tasks. If a task already exists for a message, subsequent reactions are ignored rather than creating a duplicate.

discord-trello.tagline: Track community projects on Trello straight from Discord channels
discord-trello.metaTitle: Discord + Trello Automation - Community Project Tracking | GAIA
discord-trello.metaDescription: Connect Discord and Trello with GAIA. Create Trello cards from Discord messages, post card updates to Discord, and keep community projects moving without leaving chat.
discord-trello.intro:
Open-source communities and creator teams often coordinate on Discord but track work on Trello. The gap between them means cards get created late, assignees miss updates, and contributors don't know what's been completed until they check the board manually.

GAIA bridges Discord and Trello so community members can create and update Trello cards directly from Discord, and Trello activity posts back to the relevant Discord channel. Your community's kanban board stays current without anyone having to leave the conversation.

useCases.title: Create Trello cards from Discord messages
useCases.description: A reaction or slash command in Discord triggers GAIA to create a Trello card with the message content, assigned list, and due date. Contributors can submit work items without opening Trello.

useCases.title: Post Trello card updates to Discord
useCases.description: When a card moves to 'Done' or a comment is added in Trello, GAIA posts an update to the linked Discord channel. The team celebrates wins and stays aware of progress in real time.

useCases.title: Sync Discord feature requests to a Trello backlog
useCases.description: Feature requests posted in your Discord feedback channel are automatically added as Trello cards in the product backlog. Nothing is lost in chat history and prioritization can happen directly on the board.

useCases.title: Daily Trello board digest in Discord
useCases.description: Each morning, GAIA posts a summary of overdue and due-today Trello cards to your Discord project channel. Contributors know their priorities without a standup or manual board review.

howItWorks.step: Connect Discord and Trello to GAIA
howItWorks.description: Authorize GAIA with your Discord server and your Trello workspace. Link specific Discord channels to Trello boards and lists.

howItWorks.step: Configure card creation and notification rules
howItWorks.description: Choose which Discord triggers create Trello cards and which Trello events post back to Discord. Set default labels, lists, and assignees for each channel mapping.

howItWorks.step: GAIA keeps Discord and Trello in sync
howItWorks.description: GAIA handles card creation and status updates automatically. Your community gets real-time project visibility without switching between Discord and Trello.

faqs.question: Can Discord community members who don't have Trello accounts create cards?
faqs.answer: Yes. GAIA's Discord bot acts as the Trello account for card creation. Community members only interact with GAIA through Discord reactions or commands.

faqs.question: Which Trello list does GAIA add new cards to?
faqs.answer: You configure the default list per Discord channel in GAIA's settings. You can also allow users to specify a list in the Discord command at creation time.

faqs.question: Can I limit which Discord channels can create Trello cards?
faqs.answer: Yes. GAIA only listens for card-creation triggers in the channels you explicitly configure. Other Discord channels are ignored, preventing accidental card creation from casual conversation.

discord-jira.tagline: Surface Jira issue updates in Discord engineering channels automatically
discord-jira.metaTitle: Discord + Jira Automation - Engineering Channel Issue Updates | GAIA
discord-jira.metaDescription: Connect Discord and Jira with GAIA. Post Jira issue updates to Discord channels, create Jira tickets from Discord, and keep open-source engineers informed.
discord-jira.intro:
Engineering teams that collaborate on Discord still rely on Jira for issue tracking, but context gets siloed between the two. Developers miss status changes because they're heads-down in Discord, while Jira comments go unnoticed by contributors who don't have Jira notifications configured.

GAIA bridges Discord and Jira so engineers stay informed in their primary channel. Jira issue transitions, new assignments, and sprint updates post to the right Discord channels automatically, and team members can create Jira issues directly from Discord without switching tools.

useCases.title: Post Jira issue status changes to Discord
useCases.description: When a Jira issue transitions — from 'In Progress' to 'In Review', for example — GAIA posts an update to the linked Discord channel. Engineers see real-time sprint progress without checking Jira constantly.

useCases.title: Create Jira issues from Discord messages
useCases.description: A slash command or emoji reaction in Discord lets contributors file a Jira issue instantly. GAIA pre-fills the summary, description, and project from the message context.

useCases.title: Alert Discord when a Jira bug is set to Critical
useCases.description: GAIA monitors Jira for priority escalations and posts an immediate alert to your Discord engineering channel when a ticket is marked Critical or Blocker. The right engineers are notified without delay.

useCases.title: Daily Jira sprint digest in Discord
useCases.description: Each morning GAIA posts a summary of open sprint issues, blockers, and items completed yesterday to your Discord standup channel. Teams get sprint visibility without a dedicated standup meeting.

howItWorks.step: Connect Discord and Jira to GAIA
howItWorks.description: Authorize GAIA with your Discord server and your Jira project. Map Discord channels to Jira projects so updates and alerts land in the right place.

howItWorks.step: Configure notification and creation rules
howItWorks.description: Select which Jira events — status changes, priority escalations, new assignments — trigger Discord posts. Define which Discord channels can create Jira issues.

howItWorks.step: GAIA keeps Discord and Jira synchronized
howItWorks.description: GAIA monitors both platforms and routes information according to your rules. Engineers stay informed in Discord while Jira remains the authoritative issue tracker.

faqs.question: Do contributors need a Jira account to create issues from Discord?
faqs.answer: Creating issues via GAIA requires the GAIA account to have Jira write access. Contributors trigger creation through Discord, but the issue is filed under GAIA's connected Jira identity.

faqs.question: Can GAIA notify different Discord channels for different Jira projects?
faqs.answer: Yes. You can map each Jira project to a separate Discord channel in GAIA's settings. Issues from the mobile project post to the mobile channel, backend issues to the backend channel, and so on.

faqs.question: Will GAIA spam Discord with every minor Jira comment?
faqs.answer: No. You control which Jira event types trigger Discord notifications. By default, GAIA only posts status transitions and priority changes — not every comment — to keep signal high.

zoom-asana.tagline: Turn Zoom meeting action items into Asana tasks automatically
zoom-asana.metaTitle: Zoom + Asana Automation - Meeting Action Items to Tasks | GAIA
zoom-asana.metaDescription: Connect Zoom and Asana with GAIA. Auto-create Asana tasks from Zoom meeting summaries, assign owners, set due dates, and ensure every action item gets tracked.
zoom-asana.intro:
Zoom meetings generate action items, but those action items are only as useful as the system that captures them. Without an automatic bridge, someone must manually review the recording or notes and create tasks in Asana — a step that gets skipped when everyone is busy.

GAIA listens to Zoom meeting summaries and transcripts, identifies action items, and creates Asana tasks with the right assignees and due dates before the call is even over. Your team leaves every meeting with a clear, tracked to-do list.

useCases.title: Auto-create Asana tasks from meeting action items
useCases.description: After a Zoom call ends, GAIA parses the meeting summary for action items and creates an Asana task for each one. Assignees and due dates from the conversation are applied automatically.

useCases.title: Link Zoom recording to the Asana project
useCases.description: GAIA attaches the Zoom recording URL to the relevant Asana project as a task or note. Team members can reference the recording directly from the Asana board without hunting through email.

useCases.title: Post meeting summary to Asana project updates
useCases.description: GAIA posts a formatted meeting summary — attendees, decisions, action items — as an Asana project status update so stakeholders who weren't on the call are immediately informed.

useCases.title: Create recurring tasks from recurring Zoom standups
useCases.description: For regular Zoom standups, GAIA generates recurring Asana tasks each session based on blockers and commitments raised. Sprint tracking stays current without manual entry after each meeting.

howItWorks.step: Connect Zoom and Asana to GAIA
howItWorks.description: Authorize GAIA with your Zoom account and Asana workspace. Link specific recurring Zoom meetings to the Asana projects they relate to.

howItWorks.step: Configure task creation preferences
howItWorks.description: Set which sections action items land in, how due dates are inferred, and whether GAIA should tag assignees based on who was mentioned in the meeting.

howItWorks.step: GAIA converts meetings into trackable tasks
howItWorks.description: After each Zoom call, GAIA processes the summary and populates Asana automatically. Your team leaves meetings knowing every commitment is already captured.

faqs.question: Does GAIA require Zoom AI Companion or a specific Zoom plan?
faqs.answer: GAIA works with Zoom's meeting summary and transcript features, which are available on Zoom Business and above. If your plan doesn't include summaries, GAIA can use the raw transcript instead.

faqs.question: How does GAIA determine who to assign tasks to in Asana?
faqs.answer: GAIA matches names mentioned in the meeting summary or transcript to Asana members in the linked workspace. You can review and adjust assignments before they are finalized.

faqs.question: Can GAIA handle meetings with multiple Asana projects?
faqs.answer: Yes. You can map a single Zoom meeting to multiple Asana projects, and GAIA will route action items to the correct project based on labels or keywords in the meeting notes.

zoom-linear.tagline: Post Zoom sprint summaries to Linear issues and keep engineering on track
zoom-linear.metaTitle: Zoom + Linear Automation - Sprint Meeting Summaries to Issues | GAIA
zoom-linear.metaDescription: Connect Zoom and Linear with GAIA. Auto-post sprint meeting summaries to Linear, create issues from action items, and keep your engineering cycle updated after every call.
zoom-linear.intro:
Engineering sprint meetings happen in Zoom, but the decisions and commitments made there need to live in Linear where the actual work is tracked. When that bridge is manual, sprint issues go un-updated, blockers stay undocumented, and the Linear board drifts from reality.

GAIA connects Zoom and Linear so sprint summaries are automatically posted to the relevant Linear cycle, and action items become Linear issues with proper assignees and priority. Your Linear board stays accurate after every sprint ceremony without anyone doing data entry.

useCases.title: Post sprint retrospective notes to Linear cycle
useCases.description: After a Zoom retro, GAIA formats the discussion into a structured update and posts it to the active Linear cycle. The team has a searchable record of every retro without manual documentation.

useCases.title: Create Linear issues from Zoom standup blockers
useCases.description: Blockers raised during a Zoom standup are automatically converted into Linear issues by GAIA. Each issue is assigned to the person who raised the blocker and linked to the current sprint.

useCases.title: Update Linear issue status from Zoom planning decisions
useCases.description: When a Zoom planning call moves issues between sprints or changes priorities, GAIA applies those decisions in Linear automatically. The board reflects planning outcomes instantly.

useCases.title: Attach Zoom recording links to Linear project updates
useCases.description: GAIA adds the Zoom recording URL to the relevant Linear project as a comment so engineers can replay any part of the planning or retro discussion directly from their issue tracker.

howItWorks.step: Connect Zoom and Linear to GAIA
howItWorks.description: Authorize GAIA with your Zoom account and Linear workspace. Map recurring Zoom sprint meetings to the corresponding Linear teams and cycles.

howItWorks.step: Define what gets created or updated in Linear
howItWorks.description: Choose whether GAIA creates new issues, updates existing ones, or posts cycle comments based on meeting type — standup, planning, or retro.

howItWorks.step: GAIA updates Linear after every sprint meeting
howItWorks.description: Following each Zoom call, GAIA processes the summary and applies changes to Linear automatically. Sprint ceremonies produce immediate, traceable outcomes.

faqs.question: Can GAIA distinguish between different sprint ceremony types?
faqs.answer: Yes. GAIA identifies meeting type by name or by the Zoom meeting ID you configure. Standups, planning sessions, and retros each trigger different Linear actions.

faqs.question: Will GAIA create duplicate Linear issues if the same blocker is mentioned twice?
faqs.answer: GAIA deduplicates by checking for existing open issues with similar titles before creating new ones. If a match is found, it adds a comment rather than a new issue.

faqs.question: Does this work with Linear's cycle (sprint) feature specifically?
faqs.answer: Yes. GAIA posts summaries and creates issues within the active Linear cycle. If no cycle is active at meeting time, GAIA queues the items for the next cycle or adds them to the backlog.

zoom-jira.tagline: Log Zoom sprint meeting summaries directly to Jira sprints and epics
zoom-jira.metaTitle: Zoom + Jira Automation - Sprint Summaries to Jira | GAIA
zoom-jira.metaDescription: Connect Zoom and Jira with GAIA. Auto-log Zoom sprint meeting outcomes to Jira, create issues from action items, and keep your Jira board current after every ceremony.
zoom-jira.intro:
Sprint ceremonies in Zoom generate commitments, decisions, and blockers that should live in Jira — but when the meeting ends, the notes often stay in someone's notebook or a shared doc that nobody reads. Jira issues go un-updated and sprint boards drift from what was actually discussed.

GAIA closes this gap by processing Zoom meeting summaries and logging outcomes directly to the corresponding Jira sprint. Action items become Jira issues, blockers get flagged, and sprint updates are posted before the team has even closed their laptops.

useCases.title: Create Jira issues from Zoom meeting action items
useCases.description: GAIA parses Zoom call summaries for action items and creates Jira issues in the active sprint. Each issue includes the description, assignee, and due date captured from the meeting.

useCases.title: Post sprint planning outcomes to Jira epics
useCases.description: After a Zoom planning session, GAIA logs the scope decisions and story point commitments as Jira epic comments. Product managers have a clear record of what was committed and why.

useCases.title: Flag Jira blockers surfaced in Zoom standups
useCases.description: Blockers mentioned in a Zoom standup trigger GAIA to set the Blocker flag on the corresponding Jira issue. Scrum masters see impediments updated in real time without manual triage.

useCases.title: Attach Zoom recording to Jira sprint
useCases.description: GAIA adds the Zoom recording URL as a link on the active Jira sprint board. Stakeholders can review any ceremony recording directly from Jira without asking for the link.

howItWorks.step: Connect Zoom and Jira to GAIA
howItWorks.description: Authorize GAIA with your Zoom account and Jira project. Map your recurring Zoom sprint meetings to the corresponding Jira boards and sprints.

howItWorks.step: Configure issue creation and update rules
howItWorks.description: Define which meeting types create new Jira issues, which update existing ones, and how GAIA should infer priority and assignee from meeting context.

howItWorks.step: GAIA logs meeting outcomes to Jira automatically
howItWorks.description: After each Zoom call, GAIA processes the summary and updates Jira accordingly. Sprint boards stay accurate and every commitment is traceable to the meeting where it was made.

faqs.question: Does GAIA work with Jira Software's sprint feature specifically?
faqs.answer: Yes. GAIA integrates with Jira Software and can add issues to the active sprint, update story status, and post sprint-level comments. It works with both company-managed and team-managed projects.

faqs.question: Can GAIA map different Zoom meetings to different Jira boards?
faqs.answer: Yes. Each recurring Zoom meeting can be mapped to a separate Jira board in GAIA's settings. Frontend standups post to the frontend board; backend planning posts to the backend board.

faqs.question: What happens if Zoom's meeting summary is incomplete?
faqs.answer: If the summary is sparse, GAIA falls back to the raw transcript to extract action items. You can also review GAIA's proposed Jira updates before they are applied if you prefer a human checkpoint.

zoom-teams.tagline: Bridge Zoom and Microsoft Teams for dual-platform organizations
zoom-teams.metaTitle: Zoom + Microsoft Teams Automation - Dual Platform Bridge | GAIA
zoom-teams.metaDescription: Connect Zoom and Microsoft Teams with GAIA. Share meeting summaries across platforms, notify Teams channels of Zoom events, and keep both platforms in sync.
zoom-teams.intro:
Many organizations run Zoom for video calls and Microsoft Teams for messaging, creating a split where meeting context lives in one platform and team communication in another. Colleagues who weren't on a Zoom call miss decisions; Teams notifications go unseen by people who live in Zoom.

GAIA bridges Zoom and Microsoft Teams so meeting summaries, recordings, and action items flow into the right Teams channels automatically. Both platform users stay informed and aligned without anyone manually copy-pasting between tools.

useCases.title: Post Zoom meeting summaries to Microsoft Teams channels
useCases.description: After a Zoom call, GAIA formats the meeting summary and posts it to the designated Teams channel. Team members who weren't on the call get full context without needing to watch the recording.

useCases.title: Send Teams reminders for upcoming Zoom meetings
useCases.description: GAIA posts a reminder in the relevant Teams channel 15 minutes before a Zoom meeting starts, including the join link. Participants never miss a call because they were heads-down in Teams.

useCases.title: Share Zoom recording links in Teams
useCases.description: When a Zoom recording is ready, GAIA posts the link to the appropriate Teams channel. Stakeholders access the recording in their primary collaboration tool without hunting through email.

useCases.title: Notify Teams of Zoom meeting action items
useCases.description: GAIA extracts action items from Zoom summaries and posts a structured list to Teams. Each item shows the owner and due date, giving the team a clear view of commitments made in the call.

howItWorks.step: Connect Zoom and Microsoft Teams to GAIA
howItWorks.description: Authorize GAIA with your Zoom account and Microsoft Teams tenant. Map Zoom meetings or meeting series to the corresponding Teams channels.

howItWorks.step: Configure notification and summary preferences
howItWorks.description: Choose which Zoom events — meeting end, recording ready, action items — trigger posts to Teams and set the format and channel for each notification type.

howItWorks.step: GAIA keeps both platforms synchronized
howItWorks.description: GAIA monitors your Zoom account and posts structured updates to Teams automatically. Users on either platform stay informed without manual handoffs.

faqs.question: Can GAIA also notify Zoom participants about Teams messages?
faqs.answer: Yes. GAIA can bridge in both directions — important Teams channel messages can trigger a Zoom chat notification or email digest for participants who primarily use Zoom for communication.

faqs.question: Does this work for organizations that use Zoom Rooms?
faqs.answer: GAIA integrates with Zoom Meetings and Zoom Webinars via the Zoom API. Zoom Rooms support depends on whether your Zoom plan exposes room meeting data through the same API.

faqs.question: Is there a risk of double-notifications if users have both Zoom and Teams open?
faqs.answer: GAIA posts to Teams channels rather than individual DMs by default, so notifications appear as channel posts rather than personal pings. You can adjust per-channel notification settings in Teams to control alert frequency.

zoom-drive.tagline: Auto-save Zoom recordings and notes to organized Google Drive folders
zoom-drive.metaTitle: Zoom + Google Drive Automation - Auto-Save Recordings | GAIA
zoom-drive.metaDescription: Connect Zoom and Google Drive with GAIA. Automatically save Zoom recordings to Drive, organize by meeting type, and keep your team's video archive structured.
zoom-drive.intro:
Zoom recordings pile up in the cloud without organization, making it hard to find a specific call weeks later. Google Drive is where most teams already store their documents and shared files, yet Zoom recordings never land there automatically.

GAIA moves Zoom recordings to Google Drive as soon as they're ready and organizes them into folders by meeting name, date, or project. Your team always knows exactly where to find a recording, and Drive becomes the single archive for both documents and video.

useCases.title: Auto-upload Zoom recordings to Google Drive
useCases.description: When a Zoom recording is processed, GAIA uploads it to the designated Google Drive folder automatically. No manual downloading and re-uploading required.

useCases.title: Organize recordings by project or team folder
useCases.description: GAIA routes recordings to different Drive folders based on the Zoom meeting name or host. Client calls go to the client folder; internal standups go to the team folder.

useCases.title: Save meeting transcripts as Drive documents
useCases.description: GAIA converts the Zoom transcript into a Google Doc and saves it alongside the recording. The searchable text doc makes it easy to find specific moments without scrubbing video.

useCases.title: Share Drive folder links in meeting follow-up emails
useCases.description: After saving a recording, GAIA sends a follow-up email to meeting participants with the Google Drive link. Everyone has instant access without requesting permissions manually.

howItWorks.step: Connect Zoom and Google Drive to GAIA
howItWorks.description: Authorize GAIA with your Zoom account and Google Drive. Specify the root Drive folder where recordings should be stored.

howItWorks.step: Set folder organization rules
howItWorks.description: Configure how GAIA names and organizes folders — by meeting title, date, host, or a combination. GAIA creates subfolders automatically as new meeting series appear.

howItWorks.step: GAIA archives every recording automatically
howItWorks.description: When Zoom finishes processing a recording, GAIA transfers it to Drive and applies your folder rules. Your video archive stays organized without any manual intervention.

faqs.question: Does GAIA delete the recording from Zoom after saving it to Drive?
faqs.answer: By default, GAIA saves to Drive and leaves the Zoom cloud recording intact. You can optionally configure GAIA to delete from Zoom after a successful Drive upload to manage Zoom storage limits.

faqs.question: How long does it take for a recording to appear in Drive?
faqs.answer: GAIA uploads the recording as soon as Zoom finishes processing it, which typically takes 5–30 minutes after the meeting ends depending on length. You'll receive a notification when it's available in Drive.

faqs.question: Can GAIA handle recordings from multiple Zoom hosts in one organization?
faqs.answer: Yes. GAIA supports multiple Zoom hosts within the same organization and can route each host's recordings to a separate Drive folder based on the host's identity.

zoom-figma.tagline: Schedule Zoom design reviews from Figma milestones and share feedback automatically
zoom-figma.metaTitle: Zoom + Figma Automation - Design Review Scheduling | GAIA
zoom-figma.metaDescription: Connect Zoom and Figma with GAIA. Auto-schedule Zoom design reviews when Figma milestones are hit, post meeting feedback to Figma files, and streamline design workflows.
zoom-figma.intro:
Design reviews happen in Zoom, but the designs live in Figma — and the feedback captured in the meeting rarely makes it back to the Figma file in a timely way. Designers end up reconciling handwritten notes with verbal comments from a recording they have to scrub through.

GAIA connects Zoom and Figma so design review meetings are scheduled automatically when Figma milestones are reached, and feedback from the call is posted as Figma comments before the designer's next work session. Design and review stay tightly coupled.

useCases.title: Schedule Zoom reviews when Figma milestones are reached
useCases.description: When a Figma file is marked ready for review, GAIA schedules a Zoom meeting with the relevant stakeholders. The meeting invite includes a direct link to the Figma file.

useCases.title: Post Zoom review feedback as Figma comments
useCases.description: After a design review call, GAIA parses the meeting notes for feedback items and posts them as comments on the relevant Figma frames. Designers have structured feedback without re-watching the recording.

useCases.title: Notify Figma team of Zoom meeting recording
useCases.description: When a design review recording is ready, GAIA posts the Zoom link as a Figma file comment. Anyone who missed the call can watch the review in the context of the file being discussed.

useCases.title: Create follow-up Zoom sessions for unresolved Figma comments
useCases.description: GAIA monitors Figma for comments that remain unresolved after a review and schedules a quick follow-up Zoom session with the designer and reviewer. No action item gets forgotten between design cycles.

howItWorks.step: Connect Zoom and Figma to GAIA
howItWorks.description: Authorize GAIA with your Zoom account and Figma workspace. Specify which Figma projects and status labels should trigger Zoom meeting scheduling.

howItWorks.step: Define review triggers and feedback routing
howItWorks.description: Set which Figma milestone or status change schedules a Zoom review, and configure how GAIA formats and posts meeting feedback back to Figma.

howItWorks.step: GAIA automates your design review cycle
howItWorks.description: GAIA monitors Figma for milestone changes, schedules Zoom calls, and closes the loop by returning feedback to Figma. Design reviews happen on time with complete documentation.

faqs.question: Can GAIA post feedback to specific Figma frames rather than the whole file?
faqs.answer: Yes. If the meeting notes reference specific frame names or component names, GAIA attempts to match them to Figma frames and post comments at the frame level rather than the file level.

faqs.question: Who gets invited to the Zoom design review automatically?
faqs.answer: GAIA invites the Figma file's editors and any stakeholders you configure in GAIA's review settings. You can also specify a fixed reviewer list per Figma project.

faqs.question: Does GAIA work with Figma's branching feature?
faqs.answer: GAIA tracks reviews at the file level. If you use Figma branches, configure the milestone status on the branch file and GAIA will schedule the review for that branch's reviewers.

zoom-hubspot.tagline: Log Zoom customer calls to HubSpot CRM and keep every deal updated
zoom-hubspot.metaTitle: Zoom + HubSpot Automation - Log Customer Calls to CRM | GAIA
zoom-hubspot.metaDescription: Connect Zoom and HubSpot with GAIA. Auto-log Zoom customer calls to HubSpot contacts and deals, capture action items, and keep your CRM current after every call.
zoom-hubspot.intro:
Sales and customer success teams live in HubSpot, but their customer conversations happen in Zoom. Without an automatic bridge, call notes go un-logged, follow-up tasks get missed, and HubSpot deal timelines miss critical touchpoints that inform pipeline forecasting.

GAIA logs every Zoom customer call to the right HubSpot contact and deal automatically. Meeting summaries become CRM notes, action items become tasks, and deal stages update based on what was discussed — so your CRM reflects reality without manual data entry after every call.

useCases.title: Auto-log Zoom call summaries to HubSpot deals
useCases.description: When a Zoom customer call ends, GAIA creates a HubSpot activity log on the matching contact and deal. The summary includes attendees, key discussion points, and next steps.

useCases.title: Create HubSpot follow-up tasks from Zoom action items
useCases.description: Action items identified in a Zoom call summary become HubSpot tasks assigned to the account owner. Due dates from the conversation are applied so nothing slips post-call.

useCases.title: Update HubSpot deal stage based on call outcomes
useCases.description: If a Zoom call results in a verbal agreement or a scheduled demo, GAIA advances the HubSpot deal stage accordingly. Pipeline stages stay current without relying on reps to update manually.

useCases.title: Attach Zoom recording to HubSpot contact
useCases.description: GAIA attaches the Zoom recording URL to the HubSpot contact record. Sales managers can review calls directly from CRM without asking reps for links after the fact.

howItWorks.step: Connect Zoom and HubSpot to GAIA
howItWorks.description: Authorize GAIA with your Zoom account and HubSpot portal. GAIA matches Zoom meeting participants to HubSpot contacts by email address automatically.

howItWorks.step: Configure CRM logging preferences
howItWorks.description: Set which meeting types trigger CRM logging, how deal stage changes are inferred, and which HubSpot pipelines GAIA should update.

howItWorks.step: GAIA logs every customer call to HubSpot
howItWorks.description: After each Zoom call, GAIA creates the activity, tasks, and any deal stage updates in HubSpot. Your CRM is always current without post-call admin work.

faqs.question: How does GAIA match a Zoom meeting to the right HubSpot contact?
faqs.answer: GAIA matches participants by email address. If a Zoom participant's email exists as a HubSpot contact, the call is logged to that contact. Unmatched participants are flagged for manual review.

faqs.question: Can GAIA log calls to both a contact and an associated deal in HubSpot?
faqs.answer: Yes. GAIA logs the activity to the contact record and associates it with all open deals linked to that contact. You can configure it to target a specific pipeline if a contact has multiple deals.

faqs.question: Does GAIA work with HubSpot's free CRM or only paid tiers?
faqs.answer: GAIA works with any HubSpot tier that provides API access. The free CRM supports basic contact and activity logging. Task creation and deal stage updates require HubSpot Starter or above.

zoom-clickup.tagline: Post Zoom meeting action items to ClickUp tasks automatically
zoom-clickup.metaTitle: Zoom + ClickUp Automation - Meeting Action Items to Tasks | GAIA
zoom-clickup.metaDescription: Connect Zoom and ClickUp with GAIA. Auto-create ClickUp tasks from Zoom meeting action items, assign owners, set due dates, and keep your workspace current after every call.
zoom-clickup.intro:
Zoom meetings produce action items, but those commitments only move work forward if they're captured in the project management tool your team actually uses. For teams on ClickUp, that means someone has to manually translate meeting notes into tasks — a step that often gets deprioritized.

GAIA eliminates that step by parsing Zoom meeting summaries and creating ClickUp tasks automatically. Each action item gets the right assignee, due date, and list placement so your ClickUp workspace reflects every commitment made on the call.

useCases.title: Create ClickUp tasks from Zoom meeting action items
useCases.description: GAIA processes the Zoom meeting summary and creates a ClickUp task for each action item. Assignees and due dates from the meeting are applied, and a link to the Zoom recording is attached.

useCases.title: Post meeting summaries to ClickUp docs
useCases.description: After a Zoom call, GAIA creates a ClickUp Doc with the full meeting summary, attendee list, and decisions. Teams have a searchable record of every meeting in the same tool they use for tasks.

useCases.title: Route action items to the right ClickUp list
useCases.description: GAIA maps action items to ClickUp lists based on keywords or assignees in the meeting notes. Engineering tasks go to the dev list; marketing tasks go to the campaigns list automatically.

useCases.title: Update ClickUp task status based on Zoom follow-up calls
useCases.description: When a follow-up Zoom call closes out an open ClickUp task, GAIA marks it complete and posts a note with the call summary. Task history stays connected to the conversations that drove it.

howItWorks.step: Connect Zoom and ClickUp to GAIA
howItWorks.description: Authorize GAIA with your Zoom account and ClickUp workspace. Map Zoom meeting series to the ClickUp spaces, folders, and lists where tasks should be created.

howItWorks.step: Configure task creation and routing rules
howItWorks.description: Define how GAIA infers assignees, due dates, and priority from meeting context. Set keyword-based routing rules to place tasks in the correct ClickUp list automatically.

howItWorks.step: GAIA turns every Zoom call into ClickUp tasks
howItWorks.description: After each Zoom meeting, GAIA creates tasks in ClickUp with full context. Your team leaves every call with a complete, tracked to-do list ready to execute.

faqs.question: Can GAIA create tasks in different ClickUp spaces for different meeting types?
faqs.answer: Yes. You can configure separate ClickUp space and list mappings for each recurring Zoom meeting or meeting type. Each meeting series routes to its own ClickUp destination.

faqs.question: Does GAIA work with ClickUp's subtask feature?
faqs.answer: Yes. If an action item in the meeting summary has sub-steps, GAIA can create a parent task with subtasks. You can enable or disable subtask creation in GAIA's ClickUp settings.

faqs.question: How does GAIA handle meetings where no clear action items are identified?
faqs.answer: If no action items are found, GAIA creates a summary-only ClickUp Doc rather than tasks. You receive a notification so you can manually add tasks if the meeting contained implicit commitments.

teams-slack.tagline: Bridge Teams and Slack for seamless dual-platform collaboration
teams-slack.metaTitle: Microsoft Teams + Slack Automation - Unified Team Comms | GAIA
teams-slack.metaDescription: Connect Microsoft Teams and Slack with GAIA. Mirror messages across platforms, route alerts to the right tool, and keep dual-platform teams aligned automatically.
teams-slack.intro:
Organizations that have grown through acquisitions or departmental preferences often find themselves running both Microsoft Teams and Slack simultaneously. Messages sent in one platform go unseen by colleagues on the other, and critical decisions get siloed inside whichever tool a team happens to prefer.

GAIA bridges the gap by routing messages, alerts, and notifications between Teams and Slack automatically. Announcements posted in a Teams channel can be mirrored to the equivalent Slack channel, and Slack bot alerts can be forwarded to Teams so every stakeholder stays informed regardless of which platform they live in.

useCases.title: Mirror announcements across platforms
useCases.description: Company-wide announcements posted in Teams are automatically reposted to the corresponding Slack channel. Every employee sees the message in their preferred tool without the sender duplicating effort.

useCases.title: Route support alerts to the right platform
useCases.description: Monitoring alerts configured for Slack are forwarded to Teams channels where on-call engineers work. Incidents get the right eyes on them immediately regardless of platform preference.

useCases.title: Cross-platform meeting summaries
useCases.description: After a Teams meeting concludes, GAIA posts an AI-generated summary to the relevant Slack channel. Slack-native teammates stay in the loop without needing a Teams account.

useCases.title: Unified status updates
useCases.description: Project status messages from Slack workflows are mirrored into Teams project channels. Leadership teams using Teams always have current project visibility.

howItWorks.step: Connect Microsoft Teams and Slack to GAIA
howItWorks.description: Authorize GAIA with your Teams tenant and Slack workspace in a few clicks. GAIA securely stores credentials and begins monitoring both platforms.

howItWorks.step: Configure channel mappings and routing rules
howItWorks.description: Specify which Teams channels map to which Slack channels and set filters for message types, keywords, or senders to route. GAIA applies your rules without syncing noise.

howItWorks.step: GAIA automates cross-platform messaging
howItWorks.description: Messages matching your rules are formatted and posted to the target platform in real time. GAIA attributes the source clearly so recipients know where the message originated.

faqs.question: Will GAIA create an infinite loop of mirrored messages?
faqs.answer: No. GAIA tags mirrored messages internally and skips re-processing them. Only original messages trigger cross-platform forwarding.

faqs.question: Can I filter which messages get mirrored?
faqs.answer: Yes. You can restrict mirroring to specific channels, message types, or keywords. GAIA gives you granular control so only meaningful content crosses platforms.

faqs.question: Does this work with private channels?
faqs.answer: GAIA can access private channels in both platforms if granted the appropriate permissions during setup. You control which channels are in scope.

teams-notion.tagline: Save Teams meeting notes and decisions to Notion automatically
teams-notion.metaTitle: Microsoft Teams + Notion Automation - Meeting Notes to Docs | GAIA
teams-notion.metaDescription: Connect Microsoft Teams and Notion with GAIA. Auto-save meeting summaries, decisions, and action items from Teams to structured Notion pages.
teams-notion.intro:
Microsoft Teams hosts hundreds of meetings every week, but the knowledge shared in those calls rarely makes it into a searchable, organized format. Meeting notes live in someone's local document or get lost in chat history, making it nearly impossible to recall decisions or action items weeks later.

GAIA automatically captures Teams meeting summaries and posts them as structured Notion pages. Action items become tasks in your Notion project database, and key decisions are logged with context so your team builds an institutional knowledge base with zero manual effort.

useCases.title: Auto-generate meeting notes in Notion
useCases.description: After every Teams meeting, GAIA creates a Notion page with an AI-generated summary, attendees, and key discussion points. Teams accumulate a searchable archive of every conversation.

useCases.title: Capture action items as Notion tasks
useCases.description: Action items discussed in Teams meetings are extracted by GAIA and added as tasks in the linked Notion database with assignees and due dates pre-filled.

useCases.title: Log decisions to a Notion decision register
useCases.description: Decisions made during Teams calls are logged to a dedicated Notion database with date, context, and owner. Teams build an auditable record of how and why decisions were made.

useCases.title: Post Teams channel highlights to Notion
useCases.description: Pinned messages and important threads from Teams channels are summarized and saved to the relevant Notion project page, keeping documentation current without manual copy-pasting.

howItWorks.step: Connect Microsoft Teams and Notion to GAIA
howItWorks.description: Authorize GAIA with your Teams tenant and Notion workspace. GAIA gains access to meeting transcripts and your target Notion databases.

howItWorks.step: Configure Notion templates and mappings
howItWorks.description: Select which Notion databases receive meeting notes, tasks, and decisions. GAIA uses your existing templates so new pages match your documentation style.

howItWorks.step: GAIA automates post-meeting documentation
howItWorks.description: When a Teams meeting ends, GAIA processes the transcript, populates the Notion page, and notifies attendees with a link. Documentation is done before anyone leaves the call.

faqs.question: Does GAIA require Teams transcription to be enabled?
faqs.answer: Yes, Teams meeting transcription must be enabled in your tenant for GAIA to process meeting content. GAIA works with the transcript data Teams generates.

faqs.question: Can I customize the Notion page structure?
faqs.answer: Yes. GAIA supports custom Notion templates so the generated pages match your team's existing documentation format including properties, sections, and linked databases.

faqs.question: How quickly does the Notion page appear after a meeting?
faqs.answer: GAIA typically creates the Notion page within a few minutes of the meeting ending, once the Teams transcript is processed and available.

teams-github.tagline: Get GitHub PR and issue notifications directly in Teams channels
teams-github.metaTitle: Microsoft Teams + GitHub Automation - Dev Alerts in Teams | GAIA
teams-github.metaDescription: Connect Microsoft Teams and GitHub with GAIA. Post pull request updates, issue alerts, and CI status to Teams channels and keep your engineering team informed.
teams-github.intro:
Engineering teams that use Microsoft Teams for communication need GitHub activity to surface in their chat, not buried in email notifications or the GitHub web interface. Pull requests waiting for review, failing CI pipelines, and new issue reports all require fast human attention that email simply cannot deliver.

GAIA connects GitHub to Microsoft Teams so every relevant event — PR opened, review requested, CI failed, issue labeled — appears as a formatted Teams message in the right channel. Engineers spend less time checking GitHub manually and more time shipping code.

useCases.title: Pull request review notifications
useCases.description: When a PR is opened or a review is requested, GAIA posts a formatted card to the Teams engineering channel with the PR title, author, and a direct link. Reviewers see requests immediately.

useCases.title: CI/CD pipeline failure alerts
useCases.description: Failed GitHub Actions runs trigger an instant Teams alert with the workflow name, failing step, and branch. The on-call engineer can act within seconds of a pipeline breaking.

useCases.title: Issue triage notifications
useCases.description: New GitHub issues labeled 'bug' or 'urgent' are posted to the Teams support channel automatically. Triage happens in Teams without requiring everyone to monitor GitHub directly.

useCases.title: Release and deployment announcements
useCases.description: When a GitHub release is published, GAIA posts the release notes to the Teams product channel. The whole team is informed the moment a new version ships.

howItWorks.step: Connect Microsoft Teams and GitHub to GAIA
howItWorks.description: Authorize GAIA with your Teams tenant and GitHub organization. GAIA sets up webhooks on the repositories you select.

howItWorks.step: Map repositories to Teams channels
howItWorks.description: Specify which repositories send notifications to which Teams channels and which event types to include. GAIA filters noise so only meaningful events reach your team.

howItWorks.step: GAIA automates GitHub-to-Teams notifications
howItWorks.description: GitHub events matching your rules are formatted into rich Teams adaptive cards and posted in real time. Engineers get full context without leaving Teams.

faqs.question: Can I choose which GitHub events trigger Teams messages?
faqs.answer: Yes. GAIA lets you select from events including PR opened, PR merged, review requested, CI failure, issue created, and release published. You control the signal-to-noise ratio.

faqs.question: Can different repos post to different Teams channels?
faqs.answer: Yes. Each repository can be mapped to a separate Teams channel. For example, frontend PRs go to #frontend and infrastructure alerts go to #ops.

faqs.question: Does GAIA support GitHub Enterprise?
faqs.answer: GAIA supports GitHub Enterprise as well as GitHub.com. Provide your Enterprise hostname during setup and GAIA connects to your self-hosted instance.

teams-google-calendar.tagline: Mantén las reuniones de Teams y Google Calendar perfectamente sincronizadas
teams-google-calendar.metaTitle: Sincronización Microsoft Teams + Google Calendar - Programación Unificada | GAIA
teams-google-calendar.metaDescription: Conecta Microsoft Teams y Google Calendar con GAIA. Sincroniza reuniones bidireccionalmente, evita dobles reservas y mantén tu horario unificado en ambas plataformas.
keywords: Flujo de trabajo Teams Google Calendar
teams-google-calendar.intro:
Muchos profesionales viven en Google Calendar para la programación personal mientras que su empleador utiliza Microsoft Teams para las reuniones. El resultado es un calendario fragmentado donde las invitaciones a reuniones de Teams y los eventos de Google Calendar existen en silos separados, lo que facilita la doble reserva o la pérdida de reuniones por completo.

GAIA sincroniza reuniones de Microsoft Teams con Google Calendar bidireccionalmente. Las nuevas reuniones de Teams aparecen en Google Calendar automáticamente, y los eventos de Google Calendar pueden generar la creación de reuniones de Teams con el enlace de unión correcto. Tu horario siempre está unificado en el calendario que prefieres usar.

useCases.title: Agregar automáticamente reuniones de Teams a Google Calendar
useCases.description: Cuando se programa una reunión de Teams, GAIA crea un evento correspondiente de Google Calendar con el enlace de reunión de Teams incrustado. Ves todas las reuniones en un solo lugar.

useCases.title: Prevenir dobles reservas entre plataformas
useCases.description: GAIA verifica la disponibilidad en Teams y Google Calendar antes de confirmar una nueva reunión. Los conflictos se presentan antes de que causen colisiones de programación.

useCases.title: Agenda diaria de ambos calendarios
useCases.description: Cada mañana, GAIA compila tus reuniones de Teams y eventos de Google Calendar en una agenda unificada publicada en tu chat de Teams. Comienzas el día con un horario completo.

useCases.title: Recordatorios de reuniones a través de mensajes de Teams
useCases.description: GAIA envía un recordatorio de mensaje de Teams 10 minutos antes de los eventos de Google Calendar que no tienen contraparte en Teams, para que nunca te pierdas una reunión externa mientras trabajas en Teams.

howItWorks.step: Conectar Microsoft Teams y Google Calendar a GAIA
howItWorks.description: Autoriza GAIA con tu cuenta de Teams y Google Calendar. GAIA compara automáticamente las direcciones de correo electrónico de los participantes de Zoom con los registros de contactos y clientes potenciales de HubSpot.

howItWorks.step: Configurar reglas de registro y preferencias de seguimiento
howItWorks.description: Define qué eventos de calendario se registrarán en HubSpot (por dominio de asistente, calendario o palabra clave del evento) y establece la plantilla de tareas de seguimiento que GAIA crea después de cada reunión.

howItWorks.step: GAIA mantiene tu calendario unificado
howItWorks.description: GAIA monitorea Google Calendar en busca de reuniones con clientes y las registra automáticamente en HubSpot, creando tareas de seguimiento y sincronizando notas para que tu CRM refleje cada interacción de ventas.

faqs.question: ¿El enlace de unión de Teams aparecerá en Google Calendar?
faqs.answer: Sí. GAIA incrusta la URL de unión de la reunión de Teams en la descripción del evento de Google Calendar para que los asistentes puedan unirse directamente desde cualquiera de las plataformas.

faqs.question: ¿GAIA sincroniza cancelaciones y reprogramaciones?
faqs.answer: Sí. Cuando se cancela o reprograma una reunión de Teams, GAIA actualiza o elimina automáticamente el evento correspondiente de Google Calendar.

faqs.question: ¿Puedo limitar la sincronización a calendarios específicos?
faqs.answer: Sí. Durante la configuración, seleccionas qué calendarios de Google Calendar y qué calendarios de Teams participan en la sincronización, manteniendo los eventos personales y de trabajo separados si lo prefieres.

teams-asana.tagline: Bring Asana project updates into Teams and create tasks from chat
teams-asana.metaTitle: Microsoft Teams + Asana Automation - Tasks Meet Team Chat | GAIA
teams-asana.metaDescription: Connect Microsoft Teams and Asana with GAIA. Post Asana project updates to Teams channels and turn Teams messages into Asana tasks without leaving chat.
teams-asana.intro:
Project managers track work in Asana while the team communicates in Microsoft Teams, creating a gap where task updates and chat decisions live in separate places. Important action items decided in Teams never make it into Asana, and Asana deadline changes go unnoticed by teammates in Teams.

GAIA closes this gap by posting Asana project milestones and task updates to Teams channels and by letting team members create Asana tasks directly from Teams messages. Work gets captured where decisions are made and tracked where projects are managed.

useCases.title: Post Asana milestone updates to Teams
useCases.description: When a project milestone is completed in Asana, GAIA posts a celebration update to the Teams project channel. The whole team sees progress without needing to open Asana.

useCases.title: Create Asana tasks from Teams messages
useCases.description: React to any Teams message with a designated emoji or use a slash command to have GAIA create an Asana task from it. Action items are captured instantly without leaving the conversation.

useCases.title: Deadline approaching alerts
useCases.description: GAIA monitors Asana tasks approaching their due date and posts a warning to the Teams channel. Assignees are tagged so nothing slips past a deadline unnoticed.

useCases.title: Daily standup digest from Asana
useCases.description: Before standup each morning, GAIA posts a digest of each team member's Asana tasks due today to the Teams standup channel. The team arrives at standup already up to speed.

howItWorks.step: Connect Microsoft Teams and Asana to GAIA
howItWorks.description: Authorize GAIA with your Teams tenant and Asana workspace. GAIA links your Asana projects to their corresponding Teams channels.

howItWorks.step: Configure update triggers and task creation rules
howItWorks.description: Choose which Asana events post to Teams and how Teams message commands map to Asana projects. GAIA gives you per-project control.

howItWorks.step: GAIA automates task and project communication
howItWorks.description: Asana events appear in Teams as formatted cards with direct links, and Teams-originated tasks are created in Asana with full context. Both tools stay current automatically.

faqs.question: Can any Teams member create Asana tasks, or only admins?
faqs.answer: Any Teams member whose Asana account is connected to GAIA can create tasks from Teams messages. You can restrict this to specific roles if needed.

faqs.question: Which Asana events can trigger Teams messages?
faqs.answer: GAIA supports task completion, milestone achieved, due date approaching, task assigned, and project status changed as trigger events. You choose which ones to enable.

faqs.question: Does GAIA support Asana portfolios and goals?
faqs.answer: Yes. GAIA can post portfolio-level status updates and goal progress to Teams channels so leadership gets high-level visibility without drilling into Asana.

drive-slack.tagline: Share Drive files in Slack and auto-save Slack files to Drive
drive-slack.metaTitle: Google Drive + Slack Automation - File Sharing Made Easy | GAIA
drive-slack.metaDescription: Connect Google Drive and Slack with GAIA. Share Drive files with proper permissions in Slack channels and automatically save important Slack files to Drive folders.
drive-slack.intro:
Teams share files in Slack constantly, but those attachments disappear into Slack's storage with no organization and limited searchability. Meanwhile, important Google Drive documents are shared via long links in chat without proper access control, leaving colleagues unable to open them.

GAIA connects Google Drive and Slack so file sharing becomes effortless and organized. Files shared in Slack can be automatically saved to the right Drive folder, and Drive file links shared in Slack are enriched with previews and access-checked links. Your team's documents stay organized in Drive while the sharing experience lives in Slack.

useCases.title: Auto-save Slack file uploads to Drive
useCases.description: Files uploaded to designated Slack channels are automatically saved to the corresponding Google Drive folder. Attachments are organized and searchable rather than lost in Slack storage.

useCases.title: Share Drive files with correct permissions
useCases.description: When you ask GAIA to share a Drive file in a Slack channel, it checks and sets view permissions for all channel members before posting. No more 'request access' errors.

useCases.title: Drive file change notifications in Slack
useCases.description: When a collaborator edits a shared Drive document, GAIA posts a notification to the relevant Slack channel. Reviewers are alerted the moment new content is ready.

useCases.title: Search Drive from Slack
useCases.description: Ask GAIA in any Slack channel to find a Drive document by name or topic. GAIA searches your Drive and returns a direct link without you ever leaving Slack.

howItWorks.step: Connect Google Drive and Slack to GAIA
howItWorks.description: Authorize GAIA with your Google Drive and Slack workspace. GAIA gets the permissions needed to read, write, and manage sharing settings on Drive files.

howItWorks.step: Configure folder mappings and channel rules
howItWorks.description: Map Slack channels to Google Drive folders and set rules for which file types to auto-save. GAIA applies your organization structure automatically.

howItWorks.step: GAIA automates file sharing and organization
howItWorks.description: Files flow between Slack and Drive according to your rules. Uploads are organized, links are permission-checked, and change alerts keep the team informed.

faqs.question: Does GAIA save every file shared in Slack to Drive?
faqs.answer: No. You configure which channels and file types trigger auto-save. GAIA only moves files that match your defined rules, avoiding clutter.

faqs.question: Can GAIA set specific sharing permissions on Drive files?
faqs.answer: Yes. GAIA can set viewer, commenter, or editor permissions for individuals or entire Slack channels when sharing a Drive file via chat.

faqs.question: What file types are supported?
faqs.answer: GAIA supports all Google Drive native file types (Docs, Sheets, Slides) as well as uploaded files including PDFs, images, and Office documents.

drive-notion.tagline: Attach Drive files to Notion pages and organize Drive by project
drive-notion.metaTitle: Google Drive + Notion Automation - Connect Docs and Pages | GAIA
drive-notion.metaDescription: Connect Google Drive and Notion with GAIA. Embed Drive files in Notion pages, organize Drive folders by project, and keep documentation and files in sync.
drive-notion.intro:
Google Drive and Notion serve complementary purposes — Drive stores raw files while Notion structures knowledge and projects. But teams often maintain two separate systems that don't reference each other, forcing colleagues to hunt through Drive for the file mentioned in a Notion page or vice versa.

GAIA links Google Drive and Notion so files and documentation stay connected. Drive documents can be embedded in Notion pages automatically when projects start, and new Notion project pages trigger the creation of organized Drive folder structures. Your files and your documentation always know about each other.

useCases.title: Embed Drive files in Notion project pages
useCases.description: When a new Drive document is added to a project folder, GAIA attaches it to the corresponding Notion project page. Team members find all relevant files without leaving Notion.

useCases.title: Auto-create Drive folders from Notion projects
useCases.description: When a new project page is created in Notion, GAIA creates a structured Drive folder hierarchy for that project. File organization mirrors your project structure from day one.

useCases.title: Sync document status between Drive and Notion
useCases.description: When a Drive document is moved to a 'Final' folder, GAIA updates the linked Notion page status to 'Completed.' Document and project status stay consistent automatically.

useCases.title: Aggregate Drive files into Notion dashboards
useCases.description: GAIA scans Drive folders and populates a Notion database with file names, owners, and links. Teams get a searchable index of all project documents inside Notion.

howItWorks.step: Connect Google Drive and Notion to GAIA
howItWorks.description: Authorize GAIA with your Google Drive and Notion workspace. GAIA maps your Drive folder structure to your Notion database schema.

howItWorks.step: Configure project and folder mappings
howItWorks.description: Define how Notion projects correspond to Drive folders and which file types to surface in Notion. GAIA uses these mappings to keep both systems aligned.

howItWorks.step: GAIA automates file-to-documentation linking
howItWorks.description: New files, folder changes, and document status updates flow between Drive and Notion according to your rules. Both systems reflect the same project reality.

faqs.question: Can GAIA embed a Drive file preview inside a Notion page?
faqs.answer: Yes. GAIA embeds Drive files as Notion embed blocks so collaborators can preview documents directly within the Notion page without downloading.

faqs.question: Does GAIA create sub-folders or just top-level folders?
faqs.answer: GAIA can create multi-level folder hierarchies in Drive based on templates you define. For example, each Notion project gets Assets, Deliverables, and Archive sub-folders automatically.

faqs.question: What happens if a Drive file is deleted?
faqs.answer: GAIA can detect Drive file deletions and update the linked Notion page to mark the attachment as unavailable, keeping Notion documentation accurate.

drive-asana.tagline: Attach Drive documents to Asana tasks and keep project files organized
drive-asana.metaTitle: Google Drive + Asana Automation - Files Meet Task Management | GAIA
drive-asana.metaDescription: Connect Google Drive and Asana with GAIA. Attach relevant Drive files to Asana tasks automatically, create Drive folders for new projects, and keep work organized.
drive-asana.intro:
Asana tasks often reference documents stored in Google Drive, but linking them manually is tedious and easy to forget. Team members end up hunting through Drive to find the brief, spec, or design file associated with a task they're working on, wasting time that should go toward actual work.

GAIA connects Google Drive and Asana so relevant files are attached to tasks automatically. When a new Asana project is created, GAIA builds the matching Drive folder structure. When a Drive document changes, GAIA updates the linked Asana task to flag the revision. Files and tasks stay connected throughout the project lifecycle.

useCases.title: Auto-attach Drive files to Asana tasks
useCases.description: When a Drive file is added to a project folder, GAIA attaches it to the relevant Asana task automatically. Team members find everything they need directly on the task card.

useCases.title: Create Drive folders when Asana projects launch
useCases.description: New Asana projects trigger GAIA to create a corresponding Drive folder with standard sub-folders. File organization is consistent from the first day of every project.

useCases.title: Flag document revisions on Asana tasks
useCases.description: When an attached Drive document is edited, GAIA posts a comment on the Asana task noting the change. Assignees are always aware of the latest file version.

useCases.title: Compile project deliverables in Drive at completion
useCases.description: When an Asana project is marked complete, GAIA collects all task attachments and organizes them into a final deliverables folder in Drive for easy archiving.

howItWorks.step: Connect Google Drive and Asana to GAIA
howItWorks.description: Authorize GAIA with your Google Drive and Asana workspace. GAIA links Asana projects to their Drive folder counterparts.

howItWorks.step: Define folder templates and attachment rules
howItWorks.description: Set which Drive folders correspond to which Asana projects and how file types map to task sections. GAIA uses these rules to route files automatically.

howItWorks.step: GAIA automates file attachment and organization
howItWorks.description: Files added to Drive folders are attached to Asana tasks and revision alerts are posted as comments. Project files stay organized and always accessible from tasks.

faqs.question: Does GAIA attach every Drive file or only specific types?
faqs.answer: You configure which file types and folders trigger task attachment. GAIA only attaches files that match your defined rules, keeping tasks clean.

faqs.question: Can GAIA set Drive file permissions when attaching to Asana?
faqs.answer: Yes. When attaching a Drive file to an Asana task, GAIA can automatically grant view access to all task assignees and followers.

faqs.question: Does this work with Asana custom fields?
faqs.answer: Yes. GAIA can populate Asana custom fields such as 'Drive link' or 'Document status' based on the attached Drive file metadata.

drive-trello.tagline: Attach Drive files to Trello cards and keep project docs accessible
drive-trello.metaTitle: Google Drive + Trello Automation - Files on Every Card | GAIA
drive-trello.metaDescription: Connect Google Drive and Trello with GAIA. Automatically attach Drive documents to Trello cards, create Drive folders for boards, and keep project files organized.
drive-trello.intro:
Trello boards give teams a visual overview of work in progress, but files related to each card still live in Google Drive without a clear connection. Team members switch between Trello and Drive constantly to find the document they need, breaking flow and causing version confusion when multiple Drive files exist for a single card.

GAIA links Google Drive and Trello so the right files are always attached to the right cards. Drive documents added to project folders are attached to corresponding Trello cards, and creating a new Trello board triggers the creation of an organized Drive folder structure. Files and cards stay connected from kickoff to completion.

useCases.title: Auto-attach Drive files to Trello cards
useCases.description: When a Drive document is added to a project folder, GAIA attaches it to the matching Trello card. Every card has its relevant files without anyone manually adding attachments.

useCases.title: Create Drive folders when Trello boards launch
useCases.description: A new Trello board triggers GAIA to create a structured Drive folder with lists matching the board's columns. File organization mirrors your workflow from day one.

useCases.title: Document revision alerts as Trello card comments
useCases.description: When an attached Drive document is edited, GAIA posts a comment on the Trello card noting the update. Card members are notified and can review the latest version immediately.

useCases.title: Archive card files to Drive on completion
useCases.description: When a Trello card moves to the 'Done' list, GAIA organizes its attached Drive files into an archive folder. Completed work is preserved and searchable.

howItWorks.step: Connect Google Drive and Trello to GAIA
howItWorks.description: Authorize GAIA with your Google Drive and Trello account. GAIA maps Trello boards and lists to corresponding Drive folders.

howItWorks.step: Configure folder templates and card attachment rules
howItWorks.description: Define which Drive folders map to which Trello boards and how file additions trigger card attachments. GAIA applies your rules consistently.

howItWorks.step: GAIA automates file-to-card linking
howItWorks.description: Drive file additions and edits are reflected on Trello cards in real time. Teams always find the latest files directly on the card without searching Drive.

faqs.question: Will GAIA attach every file in Drive to a Trello card?
faqs.answer: No. GAIA only attaches files from folders you map to specific Trello boards. You maintain full control over which Drive content surfaces on cards.

faqs.question: Can GAIA grant Drive access to Trello card members?
faqs.answer: Yes. When attaching a Drive file to a Trello card, GAIA can automatically grant view or edit permissions to all current card members.

faqs.question: Does GAIA support Trello Power-Ups in addition to direct integration?
faqs.answer: GAIA works at the API level and is independent of Trello Power-Ups. It provides automation capabilities beyond what the built-in Google Drive Power-Up offers.

figma-slack.tagline: Get Figma design updates and comment alerts directly in Slack
figma-slack.metaTitle: Figma + Slack Automation - Design Notifications in Chat | GAIA
figma-slack.metaDescription: Connect Figma and Slack with GAIA. Post design update notifications, comment alerts, and version changes from Figma to Slack channels automatically.
figma-slack.intro:
Design teams share Figma files with product managers, engineers, and stakeholders who have no reliable way to know when designs change. Important comment threads in Figma go unnoticed by developers who are heads-down in Slack, causing feedback loops to stall and designs to ship without incorporating requested changes.

GAIA connects Figma and Slack so design activity surfaces where the team already communicates. New comments, frame updates, and version publishes in Figma appear as Slack notifications in the right channel, ensuring stakeholders stay informed and feedback gets actioned quickly.

useCases.title: Comment notifications in Slack
useCases.description: When a comment is added to a Figma file, GAIA posts a notification to the Slack channel with the commenter name, comment text, and a direct link to the frame. Feedback is visible instantly.

useCases.title: Design version published alerts
useCases.description: When a designer publishes a new Figma version, GAIA notifies the product and engineering Slack channels. Stakeholders know a design is ready for review without polling Figma.

useCases.title: Frame status change notifications
useCases.description: When a frame is tagged as 'Ready for Development' in Figma, GAIA alerts the engineering Slack channel. Handoffs happen automatically without a separate message.

useCases.title: Daily design activity digest
useCases.description: GAIA posts a morning summary of yesterday's Figma activity — new comments, resolved threads, and version changes — to the design Slack channel. The team starts each day aligned.

howItWorks.step: Connect Figma and Slack to GAIA
howItWorks.description: Authorize GAIA with your Figma account and Slack workspace. GAIA monitors the Figma files and projects you specify for activity.

howItWorks.step: Map Figma files to Slack channels
howItWorks.description: Specify which Figma projects send notifications to which Slack channels and which event types to include. GAIA filters noise so only meaningful activity is surfaced.

howItWorks.step: GAIA automates design-to-chat notifications
howItWorks.description: Figma events matching your rules are formatted and posted to Slack in real time. Teams get full context including file name, frame name, and a deep link.

faqs.question: Can I receive notifications for only specific Figma files?
faqs.answer: Yes. GAIA lets you select individual Figma files or entire projects to monitor. Unrelated files produce no Slack noise.

faqs.question: Does GAIA notify the specific person mentioned in a Figma comment?
faqs.answer: Yes. If a Figma comment mentions a team member with @, GAIA can DM them in Slack so they are personally notified of the mention.

faqs.question: Can developers reply to Figma comments from Slack?
faqs.answer: GAIA can post replies back to Figma comment threads on behalf of a connected user. Responding to feedback without leaving Slack is possible with this setup.

figma-notion.tagline: Embed Figma designs in Notion pages and sync design status
figma-notion.metaTitle: Figma + Notion Automation - Designs Inside Your Docs | GAIA
figma-notion.metaDescription: Connect Figma and Notion with GAIA. Embed live Figma frames in Notion project pages, sync design status to databases, and keep documentation and design in sync.
figma-notion.intro:
Product and design teams maintain Notion pages for requirements and specifications while the actual designs live in Figma. The disconnect means that Notion documents reference outdated screenshots instead of live designs, and design status updates in Figma never reach the project tracking databases in Notion.

GAIA links Figma and Notion so designs are always embedded live in the right pages and design status flows into Notion databases automatically. When a designer marks a frame ready, the Notion feature page reflects it. When a new Notion feature page is created, GAIA creates a linked Figma file ready for design work.

useCases.title: Embed live Figma frames in Notion pages
useCases.description: When a new Figma frame is created for a feature, GAIA embeds it live in the corresponding Notion page. Stakeholders always see the current design, not a stale screenshot.

useCases.title: Sync design status to Notion project database
useCases.description: When a Figma file's status changes to 'Ready for Review,' GAIA updates the Notion project database record. Product managers see design progress without leaving Notion.

useCases.title: Create Figma files from Notion project pages
useCases.description: When a new feature is created in the Notion product database, GAIA creates a corresponding Figma file from your team's template. Design starts organized and linked from day one.

useCases.title: Log Figma version history in Notion
useCases.description: Each time a Figma version is published, GAIA appends an entry to the design history table on the Notion page. Teams maintain a clear design changelog inside their documentation.

howItWorks.step: Connect Figma and Notion to GAIA
howItWorks.description: Authorize GAIA with your Figma account and Notion workspace. GAIA maps your Figma projects to the corresponding Notion databases.

howItWorks.step: Configure embedding rules and status mappings
howItWorks.description: Define which Figma files embed in which Notion pages and how Figma status tags map to Notion database properties. GAIA maintains these links automatically.

howItWorks.step: GAIA automates design-to-documentation sync
howItWorks.description: Figma updates flow into Notion in real time. Designs are embedded live, statuses are updated, and version history is logged without manual intervention.

faqs.question: Are Figma embeds in Notion live or static images?
faqs.answer: GAIA uses Figma's embed URL to insert live, interactive embeds in Notion. Viewers see the current design and can navigate frames directly within the Notion page.

faqs.question: Can GAIA sync Figma comments to Notion?
faqs.answer: Yes. Figma comment threads can be mirrored to a comments section of the Notion page, giving non-Figma users visibility into design feedback without needing a Figma account.

faqs.question: Does this work with Figma branches?
faqs.answer: GAIA can track specific Figma branches and reflect branch status in Notion. When a branch is merged, the Notion page status updates accordingly.

figma-asana.tagline: Link Figma design deliverables to Asana tasks automatically
figma-asana.metaTitle: Figma + Asana Automation - Design Handoffs in Task Management | GAIA
figma-asana.metaDescription: Connect Figma and Asana with GAIA. Link Figma design files to Asana tasks, update task status when designs are ready, and streamline design-to-development handoffs.
figma-asana.intro:
Design handoffs are a perennial pain point between design and engineering teams. Designers mark a Figma file ready, but the corresponding Asana task for the engineer remains untouched. Engineers start development with no indication that the design has been finalized, or they build from an outdated version.

GAIA connects Figma and Asana to automate the handoff. When a Figma design moves to 'Ready for Development,' the linked Asana task is updated, the design file is attached, and the assignee is notified. The design-to-development handoff happens automatically with zero manual coordination.

useCases.title: Auto-update Asana tasks when designs are ready
useCases.description: When a Figma frame is tagged 'Ready for Development,' GAIA updates the linked Asana task status and notifies the assigned engineer. Handoffs are instant and automatic.

useCases.title: Attach Figma file links to Asana tasks
useCases.description: When a Figma file is created for a feature, GAIA attaches the Figma link to the corresponding Asana task. Engineers always have direct access to the latest designs from the task.

useCases.title: Create Asana tasks from Figma comment action items
useCases.description: When a comment in Figma is marked as a revision request, GAIA creates an Asana task for the designer with the comment text and frame link. No revision gets lost in comment threads.

useCases.title: Design milestone progress in Asana
useCases.description: As Figma frames progress through design stages, GAIA updates the Asana project milestone completion percentage. Project managers see design progress in their existing tool.

howItWorks.step: Connect Figma and Asana to GAIA
howItWorks.description: Authorize GAIA with your Figma account and Asana workspace. GAIA links your Figma projects to their corresponding Asana projects.

howItWorks.step: Map Figma status tags to Asana task states
howItWorks.description: Define how Figma status labels correspond to Asana task sections and custom fields. GAIA translates design workflow stages into project management language.

howItWorks.step: GAIA automates design handoffs and task updates
howItWorks.description: Figma status changes trigger Asana task updates, file attachments, and assignee notifications. Handoffs are complete before anyone sends a single message.

faqs.question: How does GAIA know which Figma file corresponds to which Asana task?
faqs.answer: You configure the mapping during setup by linking Figma projects to Asana projects. GAIA then matches files and frames to tasks based on naming conventions or custom tags you define.

faqs.question: Can GAIA handle multiple design revisions on the same Asana task?
faqs.answer: Yes. Each time a new Figma version is published, GAIA appends a comment to the Asana task with the version number and link. The full revision history is visible on the task.

faqs.question: Does GAIA work with Figma components and libraries?
faqs.answer: GAIA focuses on Figma files and frames rather than component libraries. Handoff automation applies to design files associated with specific product features in Asana.

stripe-slack.tagline: Get payment alerts, MRR updates, and churn notifications in Slack
stripe-slack.metaTitle: Stripe + Slack Automation - Revenue Alerts in Team Chat | GAIA
stripe-slack.metaDescription: Connect Stripe and Slack with GAIA. Post payment notifications, MRR milestones, failed charge alerts, and churn warnings to Slack channels automatically.
stripe-slack.intro:
Revenue events happen in Stripe but the team learns about them through manual dashboard checks or end-of-month reports. Failed payments go unnoticed for days, churn happens silently, and MRR milestones are celebrated after the fact instead of in real time when the energy is highest.

GAIA connects Stripe to Slack so every meaningful revenue event surfaces immediately in the right channel. New subscriptions ring the virtual sales bell, failed charges alert the support team instantly, and MRR milestone posts keep the whole company motivated and informed without anyone opening the Stripe dashboard.

useCases.title: New subscription and payment notifications
useCases.description: Every new Stripe subscription triggers a Slack message in the sales channel with the customer name and plan. The team celebrates wins in real time as they happen.

useCases.title: Failed payment alerts for immediate recovery
useCases.description: Failed charges post an instant Slack alert to the support channel with the customer, amount, and failure reason. Recovery outreach begins within minutes instead of days.

useCases.title: MRR milestone announcements
useCases.description: When MRR crosses a milestone threshold, GAIA posts a company-wide Slack announcement. Revenue milestones are celebrated the moment they are achieved.

useCases.title: Churn and cancellation alerts
useCases.description: Subscription cancellations trigger a Slack alert to the customer success team with churn reason if available. Retention outreach can begin immediately after a cancellation.

howItWorks.step: Connect Stripe and Slack to GAIA
howItWorks.description: Authorize GAIA with your Stripe account and Slack workspace. GAIA listens to Stripe webhook events and routes them to your configured channels.

howItWorks.step: Configure event types and channel routing
howItWorks.description: Choose which Stripe events post to which Slack channels and set thresholds for MRR milestone alerts. GAIA gives you per-event control over routing.

howItWorks.step: GAIA automates revenue event notifications
